{{Quote|That was a personal communiqué from Admiral Prittick of the Fleet. You all might as well hear it. His message confirms these Rebels' claims. The second Death Star has been destroyed, and Emperor Palpatine is presumed dead… as is Lord Vader. The Fleet is regrouping near Annaj.|Governor Wilek Nereus of Bakura|<ref name="TAB" />}}
'''Adye Prittick''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] serving in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|navy]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Prittick was one of the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] who survived the Imperial defeat at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], being the ranking admiral following the Imperial [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron's]] withdrawal to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Annaj/Legends|Annaj]]. He sent a personal communiqué to [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Wilek Nereus]] of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]], informing him of the destruction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] and the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Prittick's inability to determine Death Squadron's course of action led to a schism that saw several fleet officers take their forces and depart, effectively ruling out, in the admiral's mind, a renewed attack on the Rebel fleet at Endor.

==Biography==
By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="TAB" /> Adye Prittick,<ref name="EGW" /> a [[Gender/Legends|male]], was serving as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|navy]]. In that same [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="TAB" /> Prittick watched<ref name="EGW" /> as the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] dealt a severe blow to the Empire at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which saw the destruction of the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] and the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> With the Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] in disarray near the end of the battle, command fell to [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], who ordered the Imperial [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] to retreat to nearby [[Annaj/Legends|Annaj]]. Upon the fleet's arrival above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], it was determined that Prittick was the ranking admiral in command.<ref name="EGW" />

Prittick relayed news of the defeat at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], adding that the remnants of the Imperial fleet from the battle were regrouping near Annaj, to [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Wilek Nereus]] of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]]. When Nereus received this message, he revealed the transmission's contents to his dinner guests—among them, Rebels [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], who had earlier aided Bakura's Imperial forces in repelling an invasion by the [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] [[Ssi-ruu]]k. Prittick's communiqué confirmed for Nereus that the Rebels' story of the events at Endor was true.<ref name="TAB" />

While the ranking commander, Prittick was unable to determine the fleet's next course of action, leading to a schism that saw Admiral [[Blitzer Harrsk]] and other officers depart with their vessels. As a result, Death Squadron was reduced to twelve [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]—too few, in Prittick's opinion, to ensure victory should the fleet return to Endor to resume the fight. Instead, he ordered the fleet to withdraw to the planet [[Yag'Dhul/Legends|Yag'Dhul]].<ref name="EGW" />

==Personality and traits==
Though the ranking admiral of Death Squadron following the Imperial defeat at Endor, Adye Prittick was unable to develop a course of action for the fleet remnants at Annaj. This indecision was the catalyst for the fleet's subsequent breakup.<ref name="EGW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Admiral Prittick was first mentioned, in a single line of dialogue by Governor Nereus, in ''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'', written by [[Kathy Tyers]] and released in [[1994]].<ref name="TAB" /> He was given the first name "Adye" in [[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''.<ref name="EGW" />
